key_insight: CPTabKAN combines physiologically grouped EEG concepts, polynomial interaction expansion, and a Kolmogorov-Arnold-style classifier to improve interpretable MCI detection.
engineering_relevance: The method offers a clinically oriented, scalable tabular learning pipeline that preserves domain structure while exposing which EEG concepts and cross-concept interactions drive decisions.
method: Organize 1,379 EEG-derived features into 10 physiologically motivated concept groups.
Apply degree-2 polynomial transformation to capture first- and second-order concept interactions.
Use a Fourier-parameterized TabKAN classifier for nonlinear prediction and concept importance analysis.
evidence_signal: On the Study of Osteoporotic Fractures cohort (372 subjects), CPTabKAN-Second Order reached a weighted F1-score of 0.9038 in 10-fold cross-validation, outperforming GradientBoosting by 5.65 percentage points.
watch_item: Results depend on careful concept grouping and cross-validation design, and the added interaction modeling may be sensitive to class imbalance and cohort-specific feature distributions.
paper_title: Interpretable Concept-Guided Polynomial Tabular Kolmogorov-Arnold Network for EEG-Based Mild Cognitive Impairment Detection
